                                          INTRODUCTION

Included in this report are the 2024 loss ratios on Medicare supplement business as reported to
the NAIC.

Reporting of these loss ratios, on forms conforming to those developed by the NAIC, is required
by the federal Medicare Catastrophic Coverage Act of 1988 (Public Law 100-360). Although
major provisions of the Act were repealed one year later, this reporting requirement was left
intact. The data reported are utilized by state insurance departments to help evaluate insurers’
compliance with loss ratio standards for Medicare supplement policies. A copy of the experience
exhibit, which is used to collect the data, is included. The filing deadline for the 2024 exhibit was
March 1, 2025. This exhibit is submitted by companies filing the health, life, and
property/casualty (P/C) annual statement blanks.

This report shows direct premiums earned, market share, direct claims incurred, loss ratios, and
the number of covered lives on a countrywide basis for 4,807 filings by 335 insurers filing
Medicare Supplement Insurance Experience Exhibits with the NAIC. The report lists companies
in descending order of direct premiums earned and in alphabetical order by name. The report
also shows insurers’ experience separately for: 1) individual policies issued through 2021; 2)
individual policies issued in 2022, 20223 and 2024; 3) total individual policies; 4) group policies
issued through 2021; 5) group policies issued in 2022, 2023, and 2024; 6) total group policies;
and 7) total individual and group policies.

Also included at the beginning of the report is a table presenting aggregated data for
all companies reporting. Insurers reported total direct premiums earned in 2024 of $37.5
billion, with direct claims incurred of $31.8 billion for all Medicare supplement policies. This
resulted in a 84.5% loss ratio. A second table presents a 10-year summary of totals for the
years 2015 through 2024. This table reflects the numbers reported in prior-year Medicare
Supplement Loss Ratio Reports. Because companies report this data on a year-to-year
basis, there might be some differences between the current-year data and historical data.

The majority of the Medicare supplement insurance market is written through individual
policies. Total direct premiums earned for individual policies were $27.0 billion, with direct
claims incurred of $23.5 billion in 2024. This resulted in a 84.6% loss ratio. The loss ratio for
individual policies issued through 2021 was slightly lower, at 83.2%. The loss ratio for
individual policies issued in 2022, 2023, and 2024 was higher, at 90.4%.

Total direct premiums earned for all group policies in 2024 were $9.8 billion, with total
direct claims incurred of $8.3 billion, resulting in a 83.9% loss ratio. The loss ratio for
group policies issued through 2021 was slightly lower, at 83.4%; whereas, the loss ratio for
group policies issued in 2022, 2023, and 2024 was higher, at 87.4%.

Beginning with the 1994 filings, companies are to report the number of covered lives for each
policy form. Total covered lives reported in 2024 for individual and group policies was just over
13.7 million. In the event that a company has exited this business or was unable to provide the
number of covered lives, zeros appear.




                                                 1
A market share report containing the top 10 companies by direct premiums earned doing
business in each state is included in this publication. A map and report with the number of
covered lives by state is included in the Summary section. The Company Index includes all 329
insurers, summary totals for each company’s premiums and the number of covered lives
for those years prior to 2021, and a summary total for premiums and the number of covered
lives for the years 2022, 2023, and 2024.


                                        Qualifications

The accuracy of the reports included in this publication is dependent on the accuracy of the
information contained in the exhibits filed by insurers. Even though the data elements used to
prepare the reports are extracted from exhibits filed with the NAIC, the NAIC cannot verify or
guarantee their accuracy.

While the NAIC exercises a great deal of care in capturing data from these exhibits and
producing these reports, as with any statistical project of a significant magnitude, errors can
occur. Consequently, the NAIC makes no representations or warranties with respect to the
accuracy of the data and statistics in this report.

  Please send any questions regarding this report to researchrequest@naic.org. Links to this
    report and other NAIC reports can be found on the NAIC website at: NAIC Publications.
